skill.name: animate
skill.description: Review a feature and enhance it with purposeful animations, micro-interactions, and motion effects that improve usability and delight.
skill.summary: Strategically add animations and micro-interactions that enhance user experience through feedback, guidance, and delight.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 21 potential security issues, all confirmed as false positives after code review. The scanner incorrectly flagged CSS code blocks in documentation as external command execution, and YAML frontmatter/markdown headers as weak cryptography. This is a pure documentation skill file containing animation guidelines and code examples with no executable content or security risks.

content.value_statement: Static interfaces feel unfinished and miss opportunities to guide users. This skill helps you strategically add motion that provides feedback, improves understanding, and creates delightful experiences without overwhelming users.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes features to identify animation opportunities that enhance user experience
Recommends specific animation types based on context, performance, and audience
Provides technical implementation guidance including timing, easing curves, and CSS/JS techniques
Ensures accessibility compliance with prefers-reduced-motion considerations
Integrates with frontend-design skill for comprehensive design principles
limitations: Requires access to the target feature codebase and context about audience/brand to provide tailored recommendations
Cannot execute animations directly—provides implementation guidance that developers must apply
Depends on frontend-design skill being available for comprehensive anti-pattern guidance
May require multiple iterations to achieve optimal animation timing and feel

use_cases.title: Enhance User Onboarding
use_cases.description: Add welcoming entrance animations and guided micro-interactions that help new users understand interface relationships and available actions.
use_cases.target_user: Product designer working on user onboarding flow

use_cases.title: Improve Form Usability
use_cases.description: Add validation feedback animations, focus states, and submission confirmations that make forms feel responsive and clear.

use_cases.title: Create Delightful Product Experiences
use_cases.description: Add purposeful motion to key interactions like successful actions, empty states, and navigation to create memorable, polished experiences.
use_cases.target_user: UX designer seeking to elevate product polish

prompt_templates.title: Add Button Feedback Animations
prompt_templates.prompt: Add hover, click, and loading states to the primary action button with appropriate timing and easing curves.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick micro-interaction enhancement for a single component

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Page Load Experience
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the landing page and recommend entrance animations that guide user attention to key content.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive animation strategy for a new page

prompt_templates.title: Improve Form Feedback
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the checkout form and add validation feedback, focus states, and submission animations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Enhancing usability of complex user input flows

prompt_templates.title: Design Animation System
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the entire application and create a cohesive animation strategy with timing, easing curves, and accessibility considerations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building comprehensive motion design system for product

output_examples.input: Add animations to our dashboard navigation
output_examples.output: I'll add smooth slide transitions for menu items, a scale effect on hover, and crossfade animations when switching between dashboard sections. All animations will respect prefers-reduced-motion and use GPU-accelerated transforms for 60fps performance.

output_examples.input: Make the success state feel more rewarding
output_examples.output: I'll add a celebratory check mark animation with a subtle scale pulse, color transition from neutral to success green, and optional confetti effect for completed actions. Timing will be 300-400ms for the primary animation with a 150ms follow-up delay.

best_practices: Always use GPU-accelerated properties (transform, opacity) instead of layout properties (width, height, top, left) for smooth 60fps animations
Test with prefers-reduced-motion media query to ensure accessibility for motion-sensitive users
Use natural easing curves like ease-out-quart or ease-out-expo instead of default CSS easing for polished feel
anti_patterns: Avoid bounce and elastic easing curves as they feel dated and draw attention to the animation itself rather than the content
Never animate everything without purpose—excessive animation creates fatigue and diminishes impact
Don't use animation durations over 500ms for feedback interactions as it creates perceived lag

faq.question: Will this slow down my application?
faq.answer: No. The skill recommends GPU-accelerated animations using transform and opacity, which run at 60fps without affecting layout performance. Complex animations are optional and can be simplified based on performance constraints.

faq.question: Do I need animation libraries?
faq.answer: Not necessarily. The skill provides CSS-only solutions for simple animations and recommends libraries like Framer Motion or GSAP only for complex, interactive sequences. Start with CSS transitions and keyframes.

faq.question: How do I handle motion-sensitive users?
faq.answer: The skill always includes prefers-reduced-motion media query support. This disables or simplifies animations for users who have expressed motion sensitivity preferences in their system settings.

faq.question: What timing should I use?
faq.answer: Use 100-150ms for instant feedback like button presses, 200-300ms for state changes like hover effects, 300-500ms for layout changes like modals, and 500-800ms for entrance animations.

faq.question: Can this work with my existing components?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill analyzes your existing codebase and recommends animations that fit your current architecture. It works with any frontend framework and provides both CSS and JavaScript implementation options.

faq.question: What if I don't have design context?
faq.answer: The skill will ask clarifying questions about your target audience, brand personality, and performance constraints before making recommendations. This ensures animations align with your product goals rather than using generic defaults.
